research.title: From Mock Session to Production Auth: NextAuth.js v5 vs Clerk (Plus Role-Based Authorization)
research.whyTrending: User-requested. Replacing a mock session system with a real auth provider is the #1 gap in server-actions tutorials; NextAuth v5 (Auth.js) vs Clerk is a constantly debated choice, and RBAC (e.g. admins can delete any task) is rarely shown end-to-end.

seo.suggestedTitle: NextAuth.js v5 vs Clerk: Production Auth for Next.js

gaps: Comparisons list features but never show swapping a mock session for a real provider
RBAC is covered separately from provider choice — nobody connects them
Pricing math is vague or outdated; hidden costs (per-org billing, bundle size) skipped
No server-action enforcement examples (middleware-only RBAC is a known hole)
strengths: Clerk docs RBAC guide has clean metadata-based roles
Auth.js migration guide is thorough on breaking changes

officialSources.note: v5 breaking changes: root auth.ts, universal auth(), AUTH_ env inference, no getServerSession, Next.js >= 14
RBAC via publicMetadata roles in session claims

expertInsights: Auth.js v5: one auth() works across Server Components, Route Handlers, middleware, Server Actions
NextAuth v5 has no built-in 2FA, passkeys, organizations, or RBAC — all manual
Clerk Pro at scale: ~$25 base + $0.02/MAU beyond free ceiling; per-organization charges grow costs fast
Clerk prebuilt components can be ~50% of app bundle
Critical RBAC mistake: checking roles only in middleware — actions are callable directly, checks must live in the action
Layered enforcement: middleware, server components, route handlers, and actions each enforce independently
commonProblems: Vendor lock-in discovered at invoice time
statistics: Clerk free tier: 50k MRU/app (sources vary 10k-50k MAU)
Clerk Pro ~$1,025/mo at ~100k MAU as of May 2026

strategy.angle: Not another feature table — a swap-the-mock guide: design a provider-agnostic session interface, then wire it to Auth.js v5 and Clerk side by side, then build the RBAC layer neither gives you (admin deletes any task), enforced inside server actions
strategy.title: NextAuth.js v5 vs Clerk: From Mock Session to Production Auth
strategy.excerpt: Your tutorial app has a mock session and a TODO comment. I wire the same task app to both NextAuth.js v5 (Auth.js) and Clerk behind one interface, compare real costs, and build the role-based authorization layer neither provides out of the box.
hooks: Contrarian: the auth provider choice matters less than the interface you hide it behind
Cost: Clerk at 100k MAU is ~$1,025/mo — self-hosted Auth.js is $0 plus your weekends
Gotcha: RBAC checked only in middleware is a hole — actions are directly callable
outline: Hosted vs self-hosted: the actual trade-off
RBAC: the layer neither gives you
